auto_approve.mechanism: always_allow_tools (execution-gating scope): Cline's always_allow list suppresses per-invocation confirmation prompts for listed tools that are already visible

enterprise_management.mechanism: Cline does not document organization-level MCP configuration management

env_var_expansion.mechanism: Cline MCP configuration does not document environment variable expansion syntax

marketplace.mechanism: mcp_marketplace: Cline has an in-IDE MCP server discovery and installation experience

oauth_support.mechanism: Cline MCP does not document OAuth 2.0 authentication for remote servers

resource_referencing.mechanism: Cline does not document @-mention or equivalent access to MCP resources

tool_filtering.mechanism: always_allow_tools (visibility scope): Cline's always_allow list controls which MCP tools are exposed in the agent's available tool set without per-invocation prompts

transport_types.mechanism: sse_transport_support: Cline supports SSE transport alongside stdio; HTTP transport support follows MCP protocol adoption

providerExtensions.name: Built-In MCP Marketplace for Server Discovery and Installation
providerExtensions.summary: In-IDE Marketplace UI for browsing and installing MCP servers directly from the MCP Servers panel.

providerExtensions.name: AI-Assisted MCP Server Setup via Conversation
providerExtensions.summary: Conversational setup flow: paste a GitHub repo URL and Cline clones, builds, and configures the MCP server with clarifying questions.

providerExtensions.name: alwaysAllow List for Auto-Approving Specific MCP Tools
providerExtensions.summary: Per-server `alwaysAllow` array of tool names that execute without user confirmation; server-scoped, tool-name-specific.

providerExtensions.name: Global MCP Mode Setting for Token Usage Control
providerExtensions.summary: Global Cline>Mcp:Mode setting in Advanced MCP Settings that tunes token usage across all MCP servers.

providerExtensions.name: SSE Transport Support for Remote MCP Servers
providerExtensions.summary: SSE transport for remote MCP servers over HTTP/HTTPS, configured via `url` + optional `headers` instead of command/args.
